steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The parenthetical clarification defines what the researchers are studying: whether biological traits are “associated with considerable variations in venom potency.” The word “considerable” is the decisive clue — the blank must describe an effect large enough to produce notable variation in potency.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘a disconcerting’: Describes an emotional reaction (unsettling), not the magnitude of an effect. Nothing in the text suggests the venom effect is emotionally troubling to the researchers. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘an acceptable’: Describes adequacy or approval, not the size of an effect. The sentence is about how big the effect is, not whether it meets a standard. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘an imperceptible’: Means too small to detect — the opposite of what “considerable variations” requires. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘a substantial’: Means large or significant, directly matching “considerable variations in venom potency.” ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
